creator: jadawin@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hello, I didn't commit anything. I ask you to patch with http://people.freebsd.org/~jadawin/net-p2p-rtgui.diff and then following http://code.google.com/p/rtgui/issues/detail?id=117. Your issue is a known issue on rtgui. If your try is ok, i will commit rtgui-2.81 with knonw patch. On Thu, 31 May 2012, Ben C. O. Grimm wrote: > I see you rolled out rtgui-0.2.81, but it hasn't changed any of the > below for me, I'm afraid .. > > > Same problem, I'm afraid. Deinstalled the old port, removed > > /usr/local/www/rtgui too. Then installed the new/patched port and put a > > backup of config.php back in place (also tried with the stock > > config.php.example): > > > > From Chromium: > > > > Server error > > The website encountered an error while retrieving > > http://192.168.2.1/rtgui/index.php. It may be down for maintenance or > > configured incorrectly. > > Here are some suggestions: > > Reload this webpage later. > > HTTP Error 500 (Internal Server Error): An unexpected condition was > > encountered while the server was attempting to fulfill the request. > > > > Apache log; > > > > 172.16.110.6 - - [30/May/2012:16:38:18 +0200] "GET /rtgui/index.php > > HTTP/1.1" 500 - "-" "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; FreeBSD amd64) AppleWebKit/536.5 > > (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/19.0.1084.52 Safari/536.5" > > > > From cli: > > > > $ php -f /usr/local/www/rtgui/index.php > > > > PHP Fatal error: Call to undefined function import_request_variables() > > in /usr/local/www/rtgui/index.php on line 23 > > > > ===================== > > > > Ran php -f against all php files in /usr/local/www/rtgui: > > > > --- > > config.php > > > > > > (no output) > > --- > > control.php > > > > > > PHP Fatal error: Call to undefined function import_request_variables() > > in /usr/local/www/rtgui/control.php on line 21 > > > > --- > > dirbrowser.php > > > > PHP Fatal error: Call to undefined function import_request_variables() > > in /usr/local/www/rtgui/dirbrowser.php on line 19 > > > > --- > > feedread.php > > > > > > > "http://www.w3.org/TR/xhtml1/DTD/xhtml1-transitional.dtd"> > > > > > > > > > > rtGui > > > > > > > > > > > >
> >

rtgui

> >
> > > >

This feed reader feature of rtGui is 'work-in-progress' - please > > don't report bugs regarding it!

    > > PHP Notice: Undefined variable: feeds in > > /usr/local/www/rtgui/feedread.php on line 106 > > PHP Warning: Invalid argument supplied for foreach() in > > /usr/local/www/rtgui/feedread.php on line 106 > >
PHP Notice: Undefined variable: feeds in > > /usr/local/www/rtgui/feedread.php on line 113 > > PHP Warning: Invalid argument supplied for foreach() in > > /usr/local/www/rtgui/feedread.php on line 113 > > > > > > > > > > --- > > functions.php > > > > (no output) > > --- > > index.php > > > > > > PHP Fatal error: Call to undefined function import_request_variables() > > in /usr/local/www/rtgui/index.php on line 23 > > > > --- > > json.php > > > > > > PHP Fatal error: Call to undefined function import_request_variables() > > in /usr/local/www/rtgui/json.php on line 22 > > > > --- > > rssfeed.php > > > > > > PHP Fatal error: Call to undefined function import_request_variables() > > in /usr/local/www/rtgui/rssfeed.php on line 32 > > > > --- > > settings.php > > > > > > PHP Fatal error: Call to undefined function import_request_variables() > > in /usr/local/www/rtgui/settings.php on line 23 > > > > --- > > view.php > > > > > > PHP Fatal error: Call to undefined function import_request_variables() > > in /usr/local/www/rtgui/view.php on line 22 > > > > =========== > > > > I don't think I have anything out of the ordinary in php.ini, and > > nothing was changed since PHP 5.3 (except commenting out incompatible > > eaccelerator stuff). > > > > HTH
